arabic: وَمَا يَعْلَمُ تَأْوِيلَهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَٱلرَّٰسِخُونَ فِى ٱلْعِلْمِ يَقُولُونَ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ
translation: “And no one knows its true meaning except God. And those firm in knowledge say, ‘We believe in it.’”

sunni.label: Sunni emphasis
sunni.body: Most Sunni scholars put a full stop after God: so only God knows what the unclear verses truly mean, and the deeply learned just believe.

shia.label: Shia emphasis
shia.body: Shia scholars read straight on: so God and the deeply learned both know that meaning, above all the Prophet and the Imams.

voiceover.hook: Two of Islam's great traditions read this verse differently — and it all comes down to where you put a single full stop.
voiceover.question: Which of these two readings resonates with you

cta_text: Which of these two readings resonates with you?
caption_desc: One verse — and scholars don't agree where the sentence ends 🌙 #quran #tafsir #shia #sunni
